title: Daily Market Digest - July 23, 2026
subtitle: US Markets Close Lower Amid Tech Spending Concerns and Geopolitical Tensions

items.headline: US Equities Decline as Tech Giants' AI Spending Weighs on Sentiment, Oil Surges on Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Major US indices closed lower today, pressured by investor concerns over increased capital expenditures from tech heavyweights like Tesla and Alphabet for AI development, which overshadowed quarterly growth. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East further fueled market anxiety, driving oil prices higher and contributing to inflation fears.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer Surges on Record Orders
items.summary: Super Micro Computer ($SMCI) saw a significant rise following news of over $60 billion in new orders and a doubled margin guidance to 15-17%.

items.headline: Hyliion Secures Major U.S. Navy Contract
items.summary: Shares of Hyliion ($HYLN) jumped after the company secured a $41.7 million contract from the U.S. Navy.

items.headline: United Rentals Exceeds Q2 Expectations
items.summary: United Rentals ($URI) climbed after reporting stronger-than-expected Q2 revenue and earnings.

items.headline: Lockheed Martin Beats Estimates, Raises Outlook
items.summary: Lockheed Martin ($LMT) rallied after beating Q2 earnings and revenue estimates and raising its full-year guidance on robust demand for defense systems.

items.headline: Rollins Plunges on Downgrade and Disappointing Earnings
items.summary: Rollins ($ROL) fell sharply following a downgrade by RBC Capital and a disappointing second-quarter earnings report.

items.headline: Albertsons Misses Outlook, Cuts Guidance
items.summary: Albertsons Cos. ($ACI) tumbled after the grocer missed analyst expectations and significantly cut its full-year outlook.

items.headline: Tesla Tumbles on Weak Q2 Profit and High AI Spending
items.summary: Tesla ($TSLA) shares dropped significantly after reporting weaker-than-expected Q2 profits, increased AI and robotics spending, and negative free cash flow.

items.headline: Alphabet Falls on AI Spending Spikes and Free Cash Flow Concerns
items.summary: Alphabet ($GOOG) sank due to concerns over a significant increase in artificial intelligence spending, negative free cash flow, and potential AI model delays.

items.headline: Utilities Lead as Tech and Discretionary Sectors Lag
items.summary: The Utilities sector notably advanced by 2.3% today, offering a defensive play amidst market uncertainty. Conversely, Communication Services declined 1.3%, Consumer Discretionary fell 0.8%, and Health Care lost 0.6%, reflecting broader caution among investors. The technology sector also experienced heavy selling pressure.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady, Manufacturing Index Shows Slower Expansion
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is widely anticipated to maintain its policy interest rate at 3.50%-3.75% next week (July 28-29), though rising oil prices and potential tariffs pose inflation risks. Meanwhile, the Kansas City Fed Manufacturing Index decreased to 9 in July from 11 in June, indicating a slower pace of expansion in the manufacturing sector.

items.headline: Nasdaq Inc. Posts Strong Q2 Earnings, Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: Nasdaq Inc. ($NDAQ) reported robust second-quarter 2026 results, surpassing analyst expectations with a non-GAAP EPS of $1.07 and a 15% increase in net revenue to $1.5 billion. The strong performance was driven by double-digit growth across all divisions and a record IPO from SpaceX.

items.headline: RTX, Lockheed Martin, and First Citizens BancShares Report Strong Q2
items.summary: RTX ($RTX) delivered double-digit sales and earnings growth in Q2 and raised its 2026 outlook. Lockheed Martin ($LMT) also posted strong Q2 sales and earnings, increasing its full-year guidance. First Citizens BancShares ($FCNCA) reported a significant increase in Q2 net income and EPS, driven by balanced loan and deposit growth.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Oil Prices Above $100 Per Barrel
items.summary: Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, surged above $100 per barrel for the first time since late May amid escalating U.S.-Iran conflict and reported attacks on Saudi tankers in the Red Sea. These tensions create significant disruption points for global energy markets.
